description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

description: When using the REST v10.04 API to retrieve the value of pfc_priorities_config and pfc_priorities_applied columns from the Interface table, the API returns NULL values irrespective of the switch configuration.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what QoS caveat is documented?

Feature Caveat: QoS - When using the REST v10.04 API to retrieve the value of pfc_priorities_config and pfc_priorities_applied columns from the Interface table, the API returns NULL values irrespective of the switch configuration.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what SNMP caveat is documented?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: BFD sessions are not supported on sub interfaces. Use a switch virtual interfaces (SVI) to configure a BFD session.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Subinterfaces?
Feature Caveat: Subinterfaces - BFD sessions are not supported on sub interfaces. Use a switch virtual interface (SVI) to configure a BFD session.

description: Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

description: In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer.

description: Symptom: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state. Scenario: During certain port flap events, STP may erroneously mark a port as Disabled/Blocked, even when the link should remain active. Workaround: Shut down all affected ports. Restart the hpe-pvstd process using the following commands: start-shell sudo bash ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep systemctl restart hpe-pvstd ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep exit exit

description: Symptom: Traffic is being dropped. Scenario: This issue occurs when NDMD learns NULL MAC via GARP from a rogue VM and then it is distributed via EVPN to remote VTEP. New VMs requesting ARP get back NULL MAC in response and traffic drop is seen. Workaround: Run clear arp on local VTEP to re-learn the ARP entry and distribute proper ARP entries.

workaround: Run clear arp on local VTEP to re-learn the ARP entry and distribute proper ARP entries.

description: Symptom: IVRL routes are treated as iBGP route in HPE-ANW switches. Scenario: When an iBGP route is received from a vsx peer and also from a different VRF via IVRL. Though both of the routes are iBGP routes having same AD (200), the route learned from vex-peer is preferred over IVRL. It is recommended to set higher local preference via route-map in direction for the IVRL route to be selected as best route always.

It is recommended to set higher local preference via route-map in direction for the IVRL route to be selected as best route always.

description: Symptom: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state. Scenario: During certain port flap events, STP may erroneously mark a port as Disabled/Blocked, even when the link should remain active. Workaround: Shut down all affected ports. Restart the hpe-pvstd process using the following commands: start-shell sudo bash ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep systemctl restart hpe-pvstd ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep exit exit

description: Symptom: Traffic is being dropped. Scenario: This issue occurs when NDMD learns NULL MAC via GARP from a rogue VM and then it is distributed via EVPN to remote VTEP. New VMs requesting ARP get back NULL MAC in response and traffic drop is seen. Workaround: Run clear arp on local VTEP to re-learn the ARP entry and distribute proper ARP entries.

description: Symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition. Workaround: Restart the VSX daemon.

Workaround: Restart the VSX daemon.

description: Symptom: Notification clients stop receiving updates, sessions drop, a core dump is recorded, and the REST service restarts. Scenario: This issue may occur when a subscription is added or removed at the same exact time notifications are being delivered. Workaround: Wait for the REST service to complete its automatic restart, then reconnect the notification clients and reapply the desired subscriptions.

description: Symptom: The port connected to the ESXi server shows as up with a disabled role and a blocking state, causing traffic interruptions. description: Symptom: IVRL routes are treated as iBGP route in HPE-ANW switches. Scenario: When an iBGP route is received from a vsx peer and also from a different VRF via IVRL. Though both of the routes are iBGP routes having same AD (200), the route learned from vex-peer is preferred over IVRL. It is recommended to set higher local preference via route-map in direction for the IVRL route to be selected as best route always.

It is recommended to set higher local preference via route-map in direction for the IVRL route to be selected as best route always.
